problem_spec.name: spaceship_titanic
problem_spec.description: Binary classification: predict whether a passenger was Transported to an alternate dimension. Dataset: Spaceship Titanic (Kaggle), 8693 training samples, 13 features. Features: PassengerId (group_num), HomePlanet (Earth/Europa/Mars), CryoSleep (bool), Cabin (deck/num/side format), Destination (3 planets), Age (float), VIP (bool), 5 spending columns (RoomService, FoodCourt, ShoppingMall, Spa, VRDeck), Name. Target: Transported (bool). ~50/50 class balance. Key feature engineering opportunities: cabin parsing (deck/side strongly predictive), group features from PassengerId (family traveling together), spending patterns (CryoSleep passengers have zero spend), age binning, interaction features. Current baseline: F1=0.7914 (logistic regression, basic features). Top Kaggle scores: ~0.81 accuracy. Use 5-fold stratified CV.
constraints: Report mean F1 and accuracy across folds
No test set leakage (test.csv is held out for Kaggle submission)
Only use packages available: numpy, pandas, scikit-learn, xgboost, scipy. Do NOT use lightgbm, catboost, pytorch, or tensorflow.
problem_spec.data_description: CSV with columns: PassengerId, HomePlanet, CryoSleep, Cabin, Destination, Age, VIP, RoomService, FoodCourt, ShoppingMall, Spa, VRDeck, Name, Transported. Missing values present in most columns. Cabin format: Deck/Num/Side. PassengerId format: GroupNum_PersonNum (people in same group are traveling together).
forbidden_patterns: Do not use test.csv for training or validation
Do not use Name as a direct feature (data leakage risk)
problem_spec.extra_context:
Data directory: /Users/jared/Projects/arborist/data/spaceship-titanic
Load with: pd.read_csv('/Users/jared/Projects/arborist/data/spaceship-titanic/train.csv')
Print metrics as: METRICS val_f1=X.XXXX val_accuracy=X.XXXX
The METRICS line must be on stdout for metric parsing.

best_config.hypothesis: Replace the Logistic Regression model with an XGBoost Classifier, using target encoding for 'Deck' and 'Side' features to capture potential non-linear interactions, one-hot encoding for 'CryoSleep', treating 'VIP' as a binary feature, and scaling numerical features ('Age', 'RoomService', 'FoodCourt', 'ShoppingMall', 'Spa', 'VRDeck') with MinMaxScaler, while retaining all other preprocessing steps from the parent model.

rounds.hypothesis: Parse the 'Cabin' feature into 'Deck', 'CabinNum', and 'Side' (P/S) and use these as new categorical features. Specifically, 'Deck' and 'Side' are expected to be highly predictive.

Implement a 'TotalSpend' feature by summing the five spending columns (RoomService, FoodCourt, ShoppingMall, Spa, VRDeck). Also, create a binary flag 'HadZeroSpend' for passengers who spent nothing across all categories, especially considering 'CryoSleep' passengers.

Replace the current Logistic Regression model with a Gradient Boosting Machine (e.g., LightGBM or XGBoost) to capture non-linear relationships and interactions between features more effectively, especially given the confirmed predictive power of categorical features like 'Deck' and 'Side'.

Create new features by parsing 'PassengerId' to extract the 'group_num' and then calculating 'GroupSize' (number of passengers in the same group_num) and 'IsAlone' (GroupSize == 1). These features should capture social dynamics relevant to transportation.
